methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $185M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
Heart Aerospace, a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Anders Forslund and Klara Forslund, has raised $185M to date. Valuation analysis draws on two applicable methodologies triangulated against a peer universe of 8 private and 5 public comparable companies in the Space & Aerospace sector, including Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $352M based on total capital raised as a valuation proxy. Peer-median anchoring across three same-stage, same-sector private comparables produces a $1.3B implied valuation, with the peer range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 64.2x across five public tickers) is not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures.

The two applicable methods yield a triangulated range of $299M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $826M. Stage progression suggests conservatism relative to peer valuations, likely reflecting early revenue or commercial execution uncertainty. The wide dispersion—driven by heterogeneous peer outcomes—reflects material uncertainty in comparable company selection and stage-specific risk pricing within the frontier aerospace market. Revenue disclosure and clearer commercial traction would materially narrow this range.
